skill.name: caveman-compress
skill.description: Compresses Markdown and plain text memory files into shorter caveman-style wording while preserving headings, code blocks, URLs, paths, and commands. It backs up the original file before writing the compressed version.
skill.summary: Compress AI memory files to reduce repeated context tokens while preserving technical details.
skill.author: juliusbrussee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown examples, regex literals, and security documentation. Confirmed risks are limited to intended behavior: Python scripts read and overwrite a user-selected file, send its contents to Claude through the Anthropic SDK or Claude CLI, and use a fixed subprocess fallback. No evidence of malicious prompt injection, shell interpolation, credential harvesting, or unauthorized file traversal was found.

medium_findings.title: User File Content Sent to External LLM
medium_findings.description: The compression flow embeds the selected file content in a prompt and sends it to Claude through the Anthropic SDK or Claude CLI. This is core functionality, not hidden exfiltration, but users may accidentally send secrets stored in Markdown or text memory files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prompt is built from original_text and sent through the SDK or CLI. This confirms external processing, while the skill purpose makes the behavior expected rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Prompt Injection Can Influence Compressed Memory Output
medium_findings.description: The selected document is placed directly inside the LLM prompt. Malicious instructions inside a memory file could try to override compression rules or alter future memory content that is written back to disk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly concatenates untrusted file text into prompts and writes model output back. I found no embedded injection attempt in the skill files themselves.

medium_findings.title: In-Place File Rewrite With Backup
medium_findings.description: The tool writes compressed content back to the original path after creating a sibling .original.md backup. This is disclosed behavior, but failed or incorrect model output can still disrupt the working memory file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backup and overwrite behavior is explicit in code and documentation. The validation and restore path reduce impact but do not remove all data integrity risk.

low_findings.title: Fixed Claude CLI Subprocess Fallback
low_findings.description: Static analysis correctly found subprocess.run, but the command uses a fixed argument list with prompt content passed through stdin. No shell=True usage or user-controlled shell interpolation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is visible and uses a list form invocation. The user file content is supplied as stdin, so command injection evidence is absent.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives in Documentation and Regexes
low_findings.description: Many reported backtick, weak-crypto, C2, SAM, and path traversal findings are examples, Markdown formatting, or regex literals. They do not execute code or access sensitive system files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain documentation snippets or parsing regexes rather than executable risky operations. No corroborating malicious behavior was found in the scripts.

dangerous_patterns.title: External LLM Call With Full File Text
dangerous_patterns.description: The selected file text is included in a prompt and sent to Claude for compression or targeted repair.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The data flow from read_text to prompt construction to call_claude is direct and easy to verify.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Overwrite
dangerous_patterns.description: The compressed output replaces the original file after a backup is written beside it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write_text calls clearly implement backup creation and replacement of the selected file.

content.value_statement: Long memory files increase repeated context cost in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows. This skill compresses prose while preserving technical details and keeping an editable backup.
actual_capabilities: Compress Markdown, text, reStructuredText, and extensionless natural language files.
Create a sibling .original.md backup before replacing the selected file.
Preserve Markdown headings, fenced code blocks, inline code, URLs, paths, and commands.
Validate compressed output for headings, code blocks, URLs, paths, and bullet count changes.
Retry targeted fixes through Claude when validation reports blocking errors.
Skip common code, config, environment, lock, and backup file types.
limitations: Requires Anthropic API access or an authenticated Claude CLI fallback.
Sends selected file content to Claude, so sensitive memory files need review first.
Only supports files up to 500 KB.
Compression style is optimized for token reduction, not polished human prose.

use_cases.title: Reduce recurring project memory cost
use_cases.description: Compress a CLAUDE.md file so repeated AI sessions load fewer context tokens.
use_cases.target_user: Developers who use Claude Code or Codex with project memory files

use_cases.title: Maintain compact team preferences
use_cases.description: Shorten coding standards, workflow preferences, and team notes while preserving concrete instructions.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering leads who maintain shared AI guidance

use_cases.title: Prepare large notes for AI sessions
use_cases.description: Compress task notes or planning documents before adding them to an assistant context window.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers and technical writers using AI assistants

prompt_templates.title: Compress a memory file
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use on a single project memory file.

prompt_templates.title: Compress project notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a Markdown notes file is loaded often by an AI assistant.

prompt_templates.title: Compress after reviewing sensitive content
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after removing secrets or private data from a memory file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress docs/preferences.md after preserving commands, paths, URLs, and code blocks exactly.

prompt_templates.title: Compress a mixed prose file carefully
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use for Markdown that contains prose, examples, and technical references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress this memory file, preserve every code block and inline command, and keep the backup for manual review.

output_examples.input: A long CLAUDE.md file with repeated project rules, preferences, and examples.
output_examples.output: A shorter memory file in direct wording, plus a .original.md backup beside it.

output_examples.input: A Markdown task list with headings, URLs, commands, and code examples.
output_examples.output: Compressed task prose with the same headings, URLs, commands, and code examples preserved.

output_examples.input: A noncompressible configuration or source code file.
output_examples.output: The file is skipped because the detector classifies it as code or config.

best_practices: Review memory files for secrets before compression because content is sent to Claude.
Keep and edit the .original.md backup, then recompress after meaningful updates.
Diff the compressed file before relying on it for recurring project context.
anti_patterns: Do not compress .env, source code, lock files, or structured configuration files.
Do not use this on files that contain private credentials or customer data.
Do not delete the backup until the compressed memory has been reviewed.

faq.question: What files can this skill compress?
faq.answer: It targets Markdown, text, reStructuredText, and extensionless natural language files.

faq.question: Does it preserve code examples?
faq.answer: It is designed to preserve fenced code blocks, inline code, URLs, paths, commands, and headings.

faq.question: Will it overwrite my file?
faq.answer: Yes. It writes the compressed version to the original path after creating a .original.md backup.

faq.question: Does it send content to an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends selected file content to Claude through the Anthropic SDK or Claude CLI.

faq.question: Can I use it without an Anthropic API key?
faq.answer: Yes. If the API key is absent, it falls back to the authenticated Claude CLI.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk level medium?
faq.answer: The main risks are external LLM processing and local file overwrite. No malicious behavior was confirmed.
